top_risks: A broad External→Internal firewall rule permits all WAN-sourced return traffic with no address or port constraints.
The guest SSID has client isolation disabled, so guests can reach each other and internal subnets.
Wi-Fi networks negotiate WPA2 rather than WPA3, and Protected Management Frames are off.

findings.title: Broad WAN→Internal ALLOW rule with no source/destination filter
findings.affected_resource: firewall policy ac9fdc9f ("Allow Return Traffic"), External→Internal
findings.remediation: This rule permits all traffic from External (WAN) to Internal with no address/port constraints. Constrain it to established/related connections, or scope it to the specific services that must be reachable.

findings.title: Guest network without client isolation
findings.remediation: Enable guest policy / L2 isolation so guest clients cannot reach one another or internal subnets.

findings.title: Adopted switch running outdated firmware
findings.remediation: Schedule a firmware upgrade; the installed build is several releases behind the latest stable.

findings.title: Wi-Fi negotiates WPA2 instead of WPA3
findings.remediation: Move to WPA3 (or WPA2/WPA3 transitional) and enable Protected Management Frames where client support allows.

findings.title: Flat topology — IoT devices share the primary VLAN
findings.remediation: Place IoT devices on a dedicated VLAN with inter-VLAN restrictions to limit lateral movement.

findings.title: Guest vouchers configured to never expire
findings.remediation: Set a finite expiry on guest vouchers so stale credentials cannot be reused indefinitely.

findings.title: Client naming includes an unescaped device label (escaping check)
findings.remediation: No action required — recorded to confirm report renders untrusted text safely (the value above must appear escaped, not executed).

not_assessable: Per-rule hit counters (not exposed on this firmware)
